keyPoints: Streams open a file and allow data to flow into/out of it.
File Classes provide more flexibility with opening, closing, and manipulating files.
Use ifstream to open an input file and ofstream to open an output file.
Use '>>' for reading input and '<<' for writing output.
Use 'ios::in' to enable input and 'ios::out' to enable output.
Use 'ios::ate' to position at the end of the file and 'ios::beg' to position at the beginning.
Use 'ios::failbit' to set the failbit when an error occurs.
Use 'clear()' to clear the failbit and 'failbit' to check for error.
Use 'get()' to get characters from the file and 'putc()' to put a character in the file.
